title: Change Manifest
description:
Structured artifact produced by an AI (or human) co-author during an engineering change.
A single manifest tracks one change across its phases; it is both produced and consumed by
AI tooling, skill workflows, and CI validation.

Links to the methodology:
  - Surfaces:        docs/surfaces.md
  - SoT patterns:    docs/source-of-truth-patterns.md (10 patterns + pattern 4a pipeline order)
  - Cross-cutting:   docs/cross-cutting-concerns.md (6 dimensions)
  - Breaking change: docs/breaking-change-framework.md
  - Rollback:        docs/rollback-asymmetry.md
  - Playtest:        docs/playtest-discipline.md
  - AI contract:     docs/ai-operating-contract.md
  - Post-delivery:   docs/post-delivery-observation.md

title.description: Human-readable title. Not marketing copy — describe the change.

phase.description: Current phase of the methodology. See docs/product-engineering-operating-system.md.

identifier.description: Git identity, ai model ID, or agent name.

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. Must be rewritten on every material change.

supersedes.description: change_id(s) this manifest replaces or extends.

depends_on.description:
change_id(s) that must be delivered BEFORE this one can be delivered.
See docs/change-decomposition.md §dependency graph.

blocks.description:
change_id(s) whose delivery is blocked until this one ships.
Dual of depends_on; both sides should be declared for graph integrity.

co_required.description:
change_id(s) that MUST deliver together (atomic group). Partial delivery
would leave the system in an inconsistent state.

part_of.description:
Parent initiative / epic id. Purely descriptive aggregation, not a
dependency relation.

strategic_parent.description:
Optional link to an external strategic artifact (ADR / RFC / OKR /
design-doc / external ticket) that motivates this change. Unlike
`part_of` (which is an internal epic ID), this field points OUTSIDE
the Change Manifest world — to a document that agent-protocol does
not define the format of. Use it when a change is a slice of a larger
strategic initiative that has its own decision document.

See docs/strategic-artifacts.md for scope (what this field is for)
and non-scope (what agent-protocol deliberately does not define).

kind.description:
Category of the strategic artifact. Use 'other' + free-form location
when the artifact is internal to an org-specific system.

location.description:
Path to the artifact (repo-relative or absolute) or URL. Must be
resolvable so a verifier can confirm the parent exists.

summary.description:
Short human-readable description of WHAT the strategic parent
requires of this change. Not a link text — a motivation sentence.

initiative_id.description:
Optional stable identifier shared across all Change Manifests
that serve the same strategic parent. Enables machine aggregation
("show me every manifest under INITIATIVE-AUTH-REWRITE-2026Q2").

surfaces_touched.description: Every surface this change leaves a trace on. See docs/surfaces.md.

custom_name.description: Required when surface == custom; cite your stack-bridge.md.

role.description:
primary:    where the change originates / SoT lives
consumer:   surface that will re-sync because of the primary
incidental: touched but not load-bearing (e.g. doc update)

uncontrolled_interfaces.description:
Third-party SDKs / OS policies / upstream APIs this change depends on.
See docs/surfaces.md "Controllable vs uncontrollable subcategories."

monitoring_channel.description: Where release notes / advisories are tracked.

sot_map.description:
One entry per informational asset this change reads/writes/depends on.
Patterns are defined in docs/source-of-truth-patterns.md:
  1  Schema-Defined
  2  Config-Defined
  3  Enum/Status-Defined
  4  Contract-Defined     (4a pipeline-order sub-type)
  5  UI-Defined
  6  Transition-Defined
  7  Temporal-Local
  8  Dual-Representation
  9  Resolved/Variant
  10 Host-Lifecycle

sync_mechanism.description: How consumers stay in sync (event, cache-invalidation, polling, manual, etc.).

notes.description: Free-form annotation (e.g. why the risk rating, known edge cases).

transition_guard.description: Required when pattern == 6. See SoT pattern 6.

ci_sync_check.description: Does CI verify editor ↔ runtime are in sync?

declaration_file.description: Where the order is canonically declared (not scattered).

classification_updated.description: Did this change introduce new error codes / categories?

cancellation_propagation_reviewed.description:
See docs/cross-cutting-concerns.md "Cancellation and context propagation."
Required true if any long-running async operation is touched.

minification_rules_touched.description: Keep / retain rules for code shrinker or name mangler tool chain.

release_build_verified.description: Did verification run against a release/AOT/minified build, not just debug?

breaking_change.description: See docs/breaking-change-framework.md. Required if any consumer could observe a difference.

level.description: L0 Additive / L1 Behavioral / L2 Structural / L3 Removal / L4 Semantic Reversal

self_assessed_vs_worst_case.description:
Per framework "Hidden traps in severity judgment": judge by worst case, not most common case.
'escalated' = originally thought lower, upgraded after analysis.

deprecation_timeline.description:
Legacy deprecation form — dates only. Prefer the richer
`deprecation` field (→ `$defs.deprecation`) for new manifests;
it captures replacement name and migration prose in addition
to dates.

deprecation.description:
Structured deprecation marker — preferred form for L3/L4
changes. Rule 2.6 of the automation-contract algorithm
accepts either `deprecation_timeline` or `deprecation`;
do not populate both.

overall_mode.description:
1 = Reversible Rollback (blue/green, flag off)
2 = Forward-Fix (hotfix release, cannot undo persisted state)
3 = Compensation Only (side effects already emitted to the world)

long_lived_client_notes.description:
Required if any iOS/Android/desktop/game-binary consumer exists.
See docs/rollback-asymmetry.md "Long-lifecycle client special handling."

evidence_plan.description:
Each surface touched (role=primary) MUST have at least one evidence item.
See docs/ai-operating-contract.md §3 "Evidence quality."

tier.description:
Severity of this evidence item. `critical` evidence must be
collected — missing or rejected `critical` evidence blocks
Phase 6 sign-off and cannot be waived into `residual_risk`.
`standard` evidence may be moved to `residual_risk` with
`conditional_approval`. If absent, treated as `standard`
(default) for backward compatibility with pre-1.8 manifests.
The Planner assigns `tier` at Phase 2 Plan; the Reviewer may
upgrade `standard` → `critical` (which triggers send-back
to collect more evidence) but must not downgrade.
See docs/automation-contract.md §Evidence tier and
docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md §Rule 2.11.

artifact_location.description: File path, URL, or stable reference. Required when status == collected.

validated_by.description: Who/what verified this (CI job name, human, another agent).

playtest.description: Required when surfaces_touched includes 'experience'. See docs/playtest-discipline.md.

composition.description: Describe coverage: devices, locales, experience levels, target vs non-target.

reference.description: Build tag, version, or doc link.

post_delivery.description: Required when phase == deliver or observe. See docs/post-delivery-observation.md.

assumptions.description: Per ai-operating-contract.md §1, mark every assumption explicitly.

escalations.description: Points where AI stopped and requested human judgment. See ai-operating-contract.md §5.

implementation_notes.description:
Append-only notes from the Implementer role. Capture deltas from plan,
discoveries during Phase 4, scope expansions flagged via discovery loop.

review_notes.description:
Append-only notes from the Reviewer role. One entry per review topic
(cross-cutting dimension, breaking-change audit, surface coverage gap).

approvals.description:
Sign-off records. At least one approval with role=human is required
before phase can transition to 'delivered'.

scope.description: What is being approved (full change / specific waiver / partial scope).

conditions.description: Any conditions attached to the approval.

handoff_narrative.description:
Free-form human-readable summary for the next operator: what shipped,
how to observe it, residual risks, rollback trigger. See docs/situational-disciplines.md#operational-surface.

knowledge_notes_touched.description:
Paths of Cross-Change Knowledge Notes (CCKN) created, extended, or
superseded by this change. Optional — a change that only *references*
existing CCKNs (without modifying them) does not fill this field.
Used for reverse-lookup: given a CCKN, trace the changes that
contributed to it. See docs/cross-change-knowledge.md.

items.description: Repository-relative path to a CCKN markdown file.

implementation_clusters.description:
Planned and executed cluster-parallel canonical Implementer
delegations per Pattern C in
skills/engineering-workflow/references/cluster-parallelism.md. Each
cluster represents a file-disjoint subset of the Phase 4 work that
one canonical Implementer invocation implements and verifies
independently. The Planner writes this field at Phase 2 plan
approval; each cluster's `status` is updated as its Implementer
is spawned (pending → in_progress) and completes (→ completed) or
halts via Discovery (→ blocked_discovery).

Optional. Full-mode only. A Full-mode change that used a single
Implementer (no cluster delegation) validates without this field.
minItems: 2 because a 1-wide "cluster" is just serial Phase 4
execution; maxItems: 4 because Reviewer cross-cluster cross-cutting
gap-check visibility degrades past 3–4 (same reason
parallel_groups.sub_agents caps at 4).

File-disjointness across clusters (no two clusters list
scope_files patterns that resolve to the same file) is a
validator-level cross-item constraint, not expressible in pure
JSON Schema.

cluster_id.description: Stable identifier for this cluster within the change.

scope_files.description:
Glob patterns identifying the file set this cluster's
Implementer may modify. The Task Prompt for the spawned
Implementer names this boundary as a hard write boundary.
Cross-cluster pair-wise disjointness is required and
validator-enforced.

scope_surfaces.description:
Optional narrative tag of which surfaces this cluster
touches. Surfaces may overlap across clusters provided
scope_files remain disjoint (e.g. two clusters both touch
the system-interface surface but in disjoint file sets).

task_refs.description: References to plan tasks this cluster covers.

evidence_refs.description:
References (by artifact_location or an evidence-plan row
identifier) to evidence_plan.artifacts[] entries this
cluster's Implementer is responsible for populating.

independence_rationale.description:
Narrative justification that this cluster is independent
from the others (no shared state, no ordering dependency,
no overlapping test infrastructure). Required — a
hand-wavy rationale signals that clusters are not actually
independent and serial execution is safer.

assigned_identity.description:
Canonical Implementer identity spawned for this cluster.
Filled by the Planner at spawn time; before spawn, the
field may be absent. Must differ from the Planner's
identity, from every other cluster's assigned_identity,
and from the Reviewer's identity-to-be (anti-collusion
transitively per docs/multi-agent-handoff.md).

status.description:
Cluster execution state. Transitions:
  pending → in_progress (at Implementer spawn)
  in_progress → completed (Implementer cleared pre-handoff
                            self-check for its cluster)
  in_progress → blocked_discovery (Implementer hit the
                                    Discovery loop; by
                                    default all other
                                    clusters also halt)
See cluster-parallelism.md §Discovery-loop handling.

parallel_groups.description:
Audit record of parallel sub-agent fan-out events within this change.
Each entry documents one fan-out: the owning canonical role and
identity, the phase in which the fan-out occurred, the sub-agents
spawned (identity + scope), and the synthesis record.

Optional — a Full-mode change that chose not to fan out, or a Lean
change (which may not fan out at all), validates without this field.
What is NOT valid is a fan-out that happened but was not recorded;
that is a contract escape at the audit layer. See
skills/engineering-workflow/references/parallelization-patterns.md
§Audit trail.

The synthesis.performed_by_identity MUST equal the owning_identity —
fan-in synthesis is never delegated per
reference-implementations/roles/role-composition-patterns.md
§The invariant.

group_id.description: Stable identifier for this fan-out event within the change.

owning_role.description: Canonical role that spawned this fan-out.

owning_identity.description: Identity of the canonical role (must match the identity in authors[] for this role).

phase.description:
Phase in which the fan-out occurred. Pattern A (surface-parallel
investigators) is typically 'investigate'; Pattern B
(specialized audit fan-out) is typically 'review'.

pattern.description:
Which canonical fan-out pattern was used.
A = Pattern A per parallelization-patterns.md §Pattern A
    (Phase 1 surface-parallel non-canonical investigators).
B = Pattern B per parallelization-patterns.md §Pattern B
    (Phase 5 specialized non-canonical audit fan-out).
C = Pattern C per cluster-parallelism.md (Phase 4
    canonical-Implementer multi-delegation). When pattern
    is C, sub_agents entries record canonical Implementer
    identities; substantive cluster data lives in the
    top-level implementation_clusters field and this entry
    is an audit breadcrumb pointing at it.
other = a Full-mode fan-out that does not fit A/B/C;
    rationale required in context_pack_summary.

context_pack_summary.description:
One-line summary of the context pack shared with the sub-agents.
The pack itself lives in session-scoped working space per
docs/phase-gate-discipline.md Rule 5a and is not persisted here.
Required when pattern == 'other' so the non-standard fan-out is
explicable at audit time.

supervision_log_ref.description:
Optional pointer to a supervision log capturing keep / discard /
stop decisions made over the lifetime of this fan-out's
sub-agent invocations. Format: a path or stable reference (e.g.
'memory/supervision-log/2026-04-26-search-p95-investigators.md').
Required by no validator when absent, but if declared, automation
contract Tier 2 verifies the path resolves. The supervision log
is a Tier 2 (project memory) artifact per
docs/ai-project-memory.md §Recommended on-disk layout; its
structure and write-side discipline are defined in
skills/engineering-workflow/references/long-running-delegation.md
§Supervision log (keep / discard / stop). Distinct from
return_location on each sub-agent: return_location is the
sub-agent's structured return; supervision_log_ref records the
canonical role's decisions about which returns were kept,
discarded, or stopped during a long-running delegation.

sub_agents.description:
The sub-agents spawned in this fan-out. minItems: 2 because a
1-wide fan-out is a serial sub-agent call, not a fan-out — use
Patterns 1–4 in role-composition-patterns.md instead. maxItems: 4
because fan-in cross-cutting visibility degrades past ~3 parallel
returns; larger groups signal an over-scoped decomposition.

identity.description:
Distinct identity of this sub-agent. Must differ from every
canonical role identity on this change
(docs/multi-agent-handoff.md §Single-agent anti-collusion
rule) and from every other sub-agent identity in this group.

scope.description:
One-line scope boundary for this sub-agent, as declared in
the context pack (e.g. 'User surface sweep', 'Security
audit against cross-cutting-concerns §Security').

return_location.description:
Optional pointer to where the sub-agent's structured return
was captured (session-scoped working space path or
equivalent). Useful for Reviewer reproduction.

capability_envelope.description:
The tool-category envelope granted to this sub-agent.
Must be a subset of the owning_role's envelope per
role-composition-patterns.md §The invariant. Typical
values: 'read+search', 'read+search+verify-shell'.

synthesis.description:
The fan-in record. synthesis.performed_by_identity must equal
owning_identity — synthesis is never delegated. A parallel_group
without a synthesis block is a composition failure, not a
completed fan-out.

performed_by_identity.description:
Must equal owning_identity. Enforced at validation
(see cross-field constraint in top-level allOf).

manifest_fields_written.description:
Names of the manifest fields the canonical role wrote from
this synthesis (e.g. ['sot_map', 'surfaces_touched'] for
Pattern A, or ['review_notes'] for Pattern B). Empty array
is a composition failure — a fan-out that wrote no fields
produced no value.

cross_cutting_gap_check.description:
The cross-cutting gap check per parallelization-patterns.md
§Cross-cutting gap check. Record that it was done, and what
it found. The specific failure mode parallelization
introduces (gaps in the intersection of sub-agent scopes)
is invisible to any individual sub-agent.

performed.description: Must be true. false here is a composition failure.

gaps_found.description:
One-line descriptions of gaps that emerged only in the
union of sub-agent findings. Empty array means none found
(which is itself a recorded outcome); omit only if performed is false.

contradictions_found.description:
Pairs of sub-agent findings that directly contradicted
each other. Contradictions are always real — one is wrong
or the question was under-specified.

suspiciously_empty_returns.description:
Sub-agent identities whose returns were empty; worth
flagging because 'zero findings' can mean trivial scope
or silent failure.

waivers.description:
Explicit exceptions to otherwise-required rules. Each waiver MUST name
a human approver (role != ai) and a rationale. See docs/automation-contract.md.

rule_id.description: Stable identifier of the rule being waived (e.g. 'evidence.primary_surface_required').

approver_role.description: AI cannot approve its own waiver. Enforced as enum single-value.

expires_at.description: Waivers must be time-bounded; indefinite waivers are forbidden.

scope.description: What the waiver applies to — a specific surface / rule / evidence item.

checklist_items_touched.description: Free-form references to docs/cross-cutting-concerns.md checklist items.

deprecation.description:
Structured deprecation marker. Attach to a manifest whose change
deprecates a field, enum value, parameter, or subsection.

Semantic superset of breaking_change.deprecation_timeline: where
deprecation_timeline carries only dates, this object also captures
the replacement (`use_instead`) and a prose migration note, which
is what downstream consumers and contract catalogs actually need
to act on the deprecation.

Rule 2.6 of the automation-contract algorithm accepts **either**
`breaking_change.deprecation_timeline` (legacy form) or
`breaking_change.deprecation` (structured form) as satisfying the
L3/L4 "must declare a deprecation path" requirement.

Introduced in schema version 1.7.0. No existing field is marked
deprecated using this object in the 1.7.0 release; the marker is
published ready for use by subsequent schema changes.

since.description:
Version where deprecation was announced. Use semver
(e.g. `1.7.0`) or the manifest's own `schema_version`
scheme if the manifest is not shipping as a semver.

remove_in.description: Planned version for full removal. Optional; omit if not yet decided.

use_instead.description: Name of the replacement field, enum value, or subsection.

migration_note.description: Short prose note explaining the migration path.

announce_date.description: Optional calendar-date mirror of `since`; matches the deprecation_timeline legacy field.

sunset_date.description: Optional calendar-date mirror of `remove_in`; matches the deprecation_timeline legacy field.

escalation_contact.description: Who to page if consumers need help migrating.
